sutta: once while the exalted 1 dropped near visali at the gable hall in mahavana general siha visited him saluted and sat down at 1 side so seated he said to the exalted 1 is it possible lord to show the visible result of giving well see her just on that i will question you in return explain the matter as it seems good to you what do you think siha there might be 2 men 1 unbelieving mean miserly cross grain the other a believer a master giver delighting in constant giving but thank you see her on whom would compassionate arahants first have compassion the unbelieving mean miserly cross-grain man or the believer the master giver who delights in constant arms giving and he said the unbeliever lord why should they first have compassion on him but the believer on him surely they would first have compassion then what do you think zeha which of the 2 would visiting arahan's first visit the believer surely lord from whom would receiving arahants first receive arms from the believer surely lord to whom were teaching arahants first teach dhamma to the believer surely lord of whom would a fair report be brought be noise abroad that means a good reporter be circulated of the believer surely lord who would approach any gathering nobles brahmins householders recluses with confidence untroubled unashamed and generous the believer surely lord and what do you think siha who on the breaking up of the body after death would arise in the happy heaven world the unbelieving mean miserly cross grain man or the believer the master giver who delights in constant arms giving the unbeliever lord why shall he on the breaking up of the body after death arise in the happy heaven world but the believer after that may arise in the happy heaven world lord these 6 visible results are given declared by the exalted 1 i go not to the exalted 1 by faith for that i just know them i lord am a giver a master giver and on me compassionate arahants first have compassion me they first visit from me they first receive me they first teach dhamma of me a fair report is noise abroad general siha is a giver a worker the sangha servant it is said [Music] any gathering i approach noble's brahman's household is recluses i approach with confidence and troubled lord these 6 visible results of giving declared by the exalted 1 i go not to the exalted 1 by faith for that i just know them but when they exhorted 1 said the giver siha the master giver on the breaking up of the body after death arises in the happy heaven world that i know not and i dare i go to the exalted 1 by faith and the buddha said thus the master giver on the breaking up of the body after death arises in the happy heaven world that's the end of the suta
commentary: so when this the buddha is as strong as described the visible results of giving [Music] and general c has it now that the first 6 results he can see to be true because it is applicable to him because he's a great giver a great person who makes a lot of offerings to the monks and but the last 1 he said that he could not see and would take rebirth in the heaven world so he said no he just believe he has to believe the buddha for that this general siha used to be 1 of the followers of the external ascetics but later he went to see the buddha and was converted by the buddha and in 1 of the sutas later will come across he invited the buddha to his house for food and he served the buddha with me and the external ascetics and were jealous that they had lost such a famous disciple such a powerful disciple and they spread around the word now that the buddha was eating meat that was purposely slaughtered for the buddha and the buddha when the buddha heard that then the buddha informed the people that they only eat meat then the buddha's disciples only eat meat with 3 conditions that they did not slaughter the animal or ask somebody to slaughter second they don't see the killing thirdly they don't hear the animal cry as he was being slaughtered and this shows that the buddha and his disciples did eat meat only that only that it had these 3 conditions now i come to suta

the believer the master giver who delights in constant giving
